_comment: REV-07 LOAD-BEARING fixture (D-35). 20 SCORED cycles in trailing-28 with engineered subtle separation on sleep_duration_prior_night: 5 worst-recovery days have sleep ~370 min vs others at ~450 min. The separation is large enough to produce p_raw ~ 0.05 for sleep_duration but other 4 candidates have no engineered signal → p_raw ~ 0.3-0.6. BH @ q=0.10 / m=5 step-up thresholds are [0.02, 0.04, 0.06, 0.08, 0.10] — smallest p_raw=0.05 needs to satisfy p_(k) <= (k/m)*q at some k. At k=3 the threshold is 0.06; if smallest sleep p=0.05 ≤ 0.06 it WOULD reject. To engineer a TRUE suppression, the worst-day sleep separation must be even subtler — use 410 min vs 450 min so p_raw is closer to 0.10-0.15, falling between thresholds. Sample-size considerations: worst n=5, other n=15.
name: weekly-pattern-fdr-suppression
